steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The students claim that opposition to the proposal stems from concerns that more car traffic would diminish Cuba Street's distinctive character. The best quotation must directly reflect that specific worry — not other objections like consultation, business impacts, or pollution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "People behind this idea hardly consulted any of us who work or live around Cuba Street. This proposal doesn't reflect our opinions.": expresses frustration about the lack of consultation, not about the street's character. ✗
  • "A lot of the businesses on Cuba Street depend on foot traffic. If fewer people walk on the street because there are more cars, those businesses might struggle.": focuses on economic impact to businesses, not on the street's distinctive character. ✗
  • "Cuba Street is one of the most unusual streets in Wellington. I would hate to see it become just like any other street.": directly identifies the street as distinctive (“unusual”) and expresses fear of losing that identity — exactly what the students claim. ✓
  • "I think Wellington needs more streets like Cuba Street, not more streets full of loud cars spewing exhaust fumes into the air.": objects to pollution and noise, not to the loss of Cuba Street's distinctive character. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
